Name: Engine Temperature Warning Light
Causes: Engine temperature has exceeded normal limits | Low coolant level | Fan malfunction | Faulty radiator cap | Coolant leaks
OtherDetails: This light indicates the engine temperature has exceeded normal limits. Drivers should check the coolant level, fan operation, radiator cap, and for coolant leaks.

Name: Battery Charge Warning Light
Causes: charging system is short of power | charging system is not charging properly | problem with the battery itself | problem with the alternator
OtherDetails: The car's charging system is short of power or not charging properly.

Name: Oil Pressure Warning Light
Causes: Loss of oil pressure | Low lubrication | Lost lubrication
OtherDetails: Immediately check the oil level and pressure.

Name: Brake Warning Light
Causes: Handbrake is on | Hydraulic pressure lost in one side of the brake system | Fluid level in the master cylinder is dangerously low due to a leak
OtherDetails: If the light is lit continuously, it indicates a loss of hydraulic pressure or low brake fluid.

Name: Transmission Temperature Indicator
Causes: Engine temperature exceeded normal limits | Low coolant level | Fan malfunction | Faulty radiator cap | Coolant leaks
OtherDetails: This light indicates that the engine temperature has exceeded normal limits. The driver should check the coolant level, fan operation, radiator cap, and for any coolant leaks.

Name: Tire Pressure Warning Light
Causes: Low tire pressure
OtherDetails: The indicator light means the pressure is low in one of your tires.

Name: Traction Control Off Indicator
Causes: TCS (traction control system) has been deactivated
OtherDetails: The text does not specify the color of the light.

Name: Security Alert Indicator
Causes: Ignition switch is locked and requires a transponder-equipped key to restart | Malfunction in the security system
OtherDetails: If the ignition switch is locked, the light will turn on momentarily and requires the proper transponder-equipped key to restart. If the light is visible when the vehicle is on, it indicates a malfunction in the security system.

Name: Steering Wheel Lock Indicator Light
Causes: Steering wheel is locked
OtherDetails: To turn off the steering lock, insert the key into the ignition and turn it to at least the first position while turning the steering wheel in either direction.

Name: Trailer Tow Hitch Warning Indicator
Causes: The tow hitch is unlocked | There’s an issue with the lighting system
OtherDetails: The color of the light is not specified.

Name: Traction Control Light
Causes: TCS (traction control system) is activated
OtherDetails: The text does not specify the color of the light.

Name: Service Vehicle Soon Indicator
Causes: Fault condition in the anti-lock (ABS) brake system | Fault condition in the traction control (TCS) system | Fault condition in the electronic suspension system | Fault condition in the brake hydraulic system
OtherDetails: This light indicates a fault condition in one of the vehicle's chassis systems. The color is not specified in the text.

Name: Side Airbag Indicator
Causes: Fault with the side airbag system
OtherDetails: The text does not specify the color of the light.

Name: Reduced Power Warning Indicator
Causes: Engine Computer has limited engine power output

Name: Seat Belt Indicator
Causes: A seat belt has not been secured for a passenger in the vehicle
OtherDetails: The driver should ensure that all passengers have their seat belts secured.

Name: Press Clutch Pedal Indicator
Causes: Clutch pedal needs to be pressed
OtherDetails: The driver should push the clutch pedal. This light typically indicates a requirement to press the clutch to perform an action like starting the engine or shifting gears.

Name: Powertrain Fault Indicator
Causes: A powertrain fault has been detected | An AWD fault has been detected
OtherDetails: Contact your mechanic as soon as possible.

Name: Power Steering Warning Light
Causes: Issue with the power steering system
OtherDetails: Power steering will be disabled until fixed.

Name: Press Brake Pedal Indicator
Causes: Need to push the brake pedal
OtherDetails: Prompts the driver to step on the brake pedal to shift gears or start the car.

Name: Parking Brake Light
Causes: Parking brake is currently engaged
OtherDetails: Indicates that the park brake is on. Driver should disengage it before driving.

Name: Overdrive Light
Causes: Overdrive system manually turned off
OtherDetails: Indicates that the vehicle’s overdrive system has been deactivated by the driver.

Name: Oil Change Reminder
Causes: Oil life has expired
OtherDetails: Indicates it is time to change the engine oil to maintain engine health.

Name: Master Warning Light
Causes: One or more warning systems have detected an issue
OtherDetails: Usually accompanied by another warning light to pinpoint the specific problem.

Name: Information Warning Light
Causes: New message stored in the information display
OtherDetails: Color will be red or amber depending on the severity. It remains on until the cause is rectified.

Name: Icy Road Warning Light
Causes: Outside air reaching freezing temperatures | Temperatures around 35°F or 3°C
OtherDetails: Warns the driver of potential icy road conditions so they can drive cautiously.

Name: Gas/Fuel Cap Indicator
Causes: Gas or fuel cap is not properly tightened
OtherDetails: The driver should pull over safely and ensure the fuel cap is securely clicked into place.

Name: ESP Fault / Traction Control Malfunction
Causes: Problem with the vehicle traction control system
OtherDetails: Indicates a fault in the Electronic Stability Program (ESP) or traction control.

Name: Electric Park Brake Indicator
Causes: Malfunction in the electric parking brake system
OtherDetails: Indicates the electric parking brake may not engage or disengage properly.

Name: Distance Warning Indicator
Causes: Vehicle in front is too close | Approaching a vehicle too quickly | Stationary obstacle in the direction of travel
OtherDetails: Warns the driver to slow down and maintain a safe following distance.

Name: Clogged Air Filter Indicator
Causes: Reduced airflow to the engine | Air filter is clogged
OtherDetails: The driver should have the engine air filter inspected and replaced to restore airflow.

Name: Child Safety Lock Indicator
Causes: Child safety lock is active
OtherDetails: Confirms that the rear door child safety locks have been successfully engaged.

Name: Check Engine / Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL)
Causes: Diagnostic systems detected a malfunction | Bulb check during engine startup
OtherDetails: If the light stays illuminated after startup, investigate the engine malfunction immediately.

Name: Catalytic Converter Warning
Causes: Catalytic converter is overheating | Catalytic converter is not operating as intended
OtherDetails: Indicates an issue with the exhaust and emissions system. Driving with this can cause damage.

Name: Brake Fluid Indicator
Causes: Brake fluid level is low
OtherDetails: The driver should check the brake fluid reservoir and refill it if necessary.

Name: Brake Pad Warning
Causes: Brake pads are worn out
OtherDetails: Indicates that the brake pads have reached their minimum thickness and need replacement.

Name: Brake Lights Warning
Causes: Defective exterior brake light bulb
OtherDetails: The driver should check the operation of all exterior brake light bulbs and replace the faulty one.

Name: Automatic Gearbox Warning
Causes: Gearbox malfunction | Transmission malfunction
OtherDetails: Indicates a problem with the automatic transmission system. The vehicle should be inspected.

Name: Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) Indicator
Causes: Malfunction in the ABS system
OtherDetails: The standard braking system will still work, but the anti-lock feature is disabled. Avoid hard braking.

Name: All Wheel Drive (AWD/4WD) Indicator
Causes: Malfunction with the AWD or 4WD system
OtherDetails: Indicates an issue with the all-wheel-drive mechanism. The car may default to two-wheel drive.

Name: Airbag Indicator
Causes: Front airbag is switched off | Fault in the airbag system | Fault in the seatbelt system
OtherDetails: The lamp will light up or flash if there is a fault. Have the safety systems inspected immediately.

Name: Airbag Deactivated
Causes: Passenger airbag is switched off
OtherDetails: Indicates the passenger airbag is deactivated. (Note: The raw text mentions a rear spoiler fault, which is likely a typo from the original website).

Name: Adaptive Suspension Dampers Indicator
Causes: Issue with the adaptive suspension dampers
OtherDetails: Indicates a malfunction in the adaptive suspension. The driver needs to contact an authorized repairer.

Name: 4WD Lock Indicator
Causes: 4WD Lock mode is manually activated
OtherDetails: Indicates that the vehicle is currently operating in 4-Wheel Drive Lock mode for maximum traction.

Name: Air Suspension Indicator
Causes: Problem with the air suspension bags | Leak in the system | Inflation issue
OtherDetails: Indicates a malfunction in the air suspension system. The vehicle may ride lower than usual or handle poorly.

Name: Low Beam Indicator
Causes: Dipped beam (low beam) headlights are turned on
OtherDetails: Illuminates to confirm that the normal nighttime driving lights are active.

Name: Lamp Out Indicator
Causes: Exterior light on the vehicle is not functioning properly | Burnt out bulb
OtherDetails: The driver should inspect all exterior lights (headlights, taillights, turn signals) and replace the defective bulb.

Name: High Beam Indicator
Causes: High beam headlights are turned on | High beam flash function is being used
OtherDetails: Indicates maximum forward illumination is active. The driver should dim the lights for oncoming traffic.

Name: Headlight Range Control Indicator
Causes: Issue detected with the headlight range control system
OtherDetails: The system that automatically adjusts the headlight aim angle may be malfunctioning.

Name: Front Fog Lights Indicator
Causes: Front fog lights are manually turned on
OtherDetails: Illuminates to confirm front fog lights are active for better visibility in poor weather conditions.

Name: Exterior Light Fault Indicator
Causes: Any exterior light on the car is not working
OtherDetails: Similar to the Lamp Out indicator. Inspect and replace the faulty exterior bulb.

Name: Auto High Beam Indicator
Causes: Auto High Beam system has automatically switched on the high beams
OtherDetails: Confirms the automatic headlight system is actively managing the high beams based on surrounding traffic.

Name: Adaptive Light System Indicator
Causes: Adaptive headlights are turned on
OtherDetails: Indicates the headlights will pivot and adjust their beam according to the steering wheel angle and vehicle speed.

Name: Side Light Indicator
Causes: Normal headlights or side lights are in use
OtherDetails: Confirms that the vehicle's marker lights or parking lights are currently illuminated.

Name: Rear Fog Lights Indicator
Causes: Rear fog lights are manually switched on
OtherDetails: Confirms rear fog lights are active to make the vehicle more visible from behind in heavy fog or rain.

Name: Rain and Light Sensor Indicator
Causes: Issue with the rain and light sensor system
OtherDetails: The automatic wipers and automatic headlights will not function. The driver must operate them manually until fixed.

Name: Windshield Defrost Indicator
Causes: Windshield defrost system is actively running
OtherDetails: Confirms the front windshield defroster or defogger is turned on to clear visibility.

Name: Washer Fluid Reminder
Causes: Windscreen washer fluid reservoir is nearly empty
OtherDetails: The driver should fill the washer fluid reservoir as soon as possible to maintain clear windshield visibility.

Name: Rear Window Defrost Indicator
Causes: Rear window defrost is actively running
OtherDetails: Confirms the rear window defroster heating grid is turned on to clear fog or ice.

Name: Low Fuel Level Indicator
Causes: Car is running low on fuel
OtherDetails: The driver will soon need a refill. Stop at the nearest gas station.

Name: Key Not in Vehicle Indicator
Causes: Key is not detected inside the car
OtherDetails: Ensure the key fob is inside the vehicle to start the engine or continue driving safely.

Name: Hood/Bonnet Open Indicator
Causes: Car hood is not closed properly
OtherDetails: The driver should pull over safely and securely press the hood down until it clicks closed.

Name: Hazard Lights On Indicator
Causes: Hazard lights are manually turned on
OtherDetails: Indicates all turn signals are flashing simultaneously to warn other drivers of a hazard or emergency.

Name: Fan Indicator
Causes: Internal ventilation or fan is in operation
OtherDetails: Confirms the climate control fan is actively running inside the cabin.

Name: Door Ajar Indicator
Causes: One or more doors are not shut properly
OtherDetails: The driver should check all doors and ensure they are fully closed before driving.

Name: Direction/Signal Indicators
Causes: Left or right turn signal has been activated
OtherDetails: Flashes to indicate the driver's intention to turn or change lanes.

Name: Car on Ramp Indicator
Causes: Vehicle is on a ramp or jack system
OtherDetails: Often indicates service mode is active or the vehicle has been lifted for maintenance.

Name: Recirculated Cabin Air Indicator
Causes: Ventilation system is recirculating air from within the vehicle
OtherDetails: Suitable for keeping outside odors out of the cabin or warming up the interior faster when it is particularly cold outside.

Name: Rear Spoiler Warning Indicator
Causes: Fault in the rear spoiler system
OtherDetails: The active rear spoiler may not deploy or retract correctly, potentially affecting aerodynamics at high speeds.

Name: Parking with Park Assist Pilot Indicator
Causes: Park Assist Pilot (PAP) is manually activated
OtherDetails: The vehicle is currently using automated steering assistance to help park.

Name: Lane Departure Warning
Causes: Vehicle is moving out of its current lane without signaling
OtherDetails: Alerts the driver to correct the steering to stay safely within the lane markings.

Name: Lane Assist Indicator
Causes: Lane assist system is switched on | Road markings are successfully detected
OtherDetails: Confirms the system is actively monitoring the road lines to help keep the vehicle centered.

Name: Key Fob Battery Low Indicator
Causes: Key fob battery is running low
OtherDetails: The driver should replace the key fob battery to ensure remote functions and keyless start continue working.

Name: Ignition Switch Warning Indicator
Causes: Issue with the ignition system | Issue with the car key | Glitch in the system | Worn out key
OtherDetails: The vehicle may fail to start or experience power issues. The ignition switch and key need to be inspected.

Name: Hill Descent Control Indicator
Causes: Hill descent system is manually activated
OtherDetails: Helps maintain a specific, safe speed while driving down steep inclines without the driver needing to ride the brakes.

Name: Forward Collision Warning Indicator
Causes: Potential forward collision is detected
OtherDetails: Alerts the driver to brake immediately or take evasive action to avoid an accident.

Name: Eco Driving Indicator
Causes: Eco driving function is manually activated
OtherDetails: Confirms the vehicle is operating in a fuel-efficient mode, adjusting engine and climate response.

Name: Cruise Control Indicator
Causes: Cruise control system is manually activated
OtherDetails: Confirms the vehicle will maintain the set speed automatically.

Name: Convertible Roof Warning Light
Causes: Roof is actively opening or closing | Roof is not fully opened or closed | Fault in the roof system
OtherDetails: If lit continuously, it means the roof cycle is incomplete or there is a malfunction in the mechanism.

Name: Brake Hold Indicator
Causes: Brake hold system is functioning and engaged
OtherDetails: Confirms the car will automatically hold the brakes when stopped, allowing the driver to take their foot off the pedal.

Name: Blind Spot Indicator
Causes: Vehicle or obstruction detected in the blind spot
OtherDetails: Alerts the driver to avoid changing lanes until the blind spot is clear.

Name: Auto Windscreen Wiping Indicator
Causes: Windscreen wipers are operating on Automatic mode
OtherDetails: Confirms the rain sensors are actively managing the wiper speed.

Name: Automatic Emergency Braking (AEB) Indicator
Causes: AEB system is manually turned off | Radar sensor or cover is blocked with dirt or snow
OtherDetails: The vehicle will not automatically brake for emergencies. The driver should clean the front sensors or turn the system back on.

Name: Adaptive Cruise Control Indicator
Causes: Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) is in operation
OtherDetails: Confirms the vehicle is automatically maintaining a set following distance from the car ahead.

Name: Winter Mode Indicator
Causes: Winter mode is manually activated
OtherDetails: Confirms the vehicle's transmission and traction settings are optimized for driving on icy or snowy roads.

Name: Start/Stop Warning Indicator
Causes: Intelligent Stop/Start system is active and engine has shut down
OtherDetails: Confirms the engine has temporarily stopped to save fuel at a standstill and will restart automatically.

Name: Speed Limiter Indicator
Causes: Speed limiter function is manually activated
OtherDetails: Confirms the vehicle is electronically restricted from exceeding the pre-set speed limit.

Name: Seat Temperature Indicator
Causes: Air flow is distributed between front side window
OtherDetails: windscreen and footwell vents
ImageName: (Note: The raw text from the site describes climate control airflow rather than seat heating, which is likely a typo on their end).

Name: Glow Plug Indicator
Causes: Engine glow plugs are warming up | Worn out glow plug (if flashing) | Issue detected in the glow plug system
OtherDetails: For diesel engines. The driver should wait until the light goes out before starting the engine. If flashing, seek maintenance.

Name: Fuel Filter Warning
Causes: Diesel fuel filter is full
OtherDetails: The driver must empty or replace the fuel filter to avoid severe engine damage.

Name: Exhaust Fluid Indicator
Causes: Diesel exhaust fluid reservoir is low
OtherDetails: The driver needs to top up the exhaust fluid (AdBlue) soon to maintain proper emissions control.

Name: AdBlue Tank Empty Indicator
Causes: AdBlue tank is completely empty
OtherDetails: The driver must fill the tank with exhaust fluid immediately. On many cars, the engine will refuse to restart if turned off.

Name: AdBlue Malfunction Indicator
Causes: AdBlue system is malfunctioning | System is filled with non-standard liquid
OtherDetails: Indicates a fault in the emissions reduction system. The vehicle requires professional servicing.

Name: Water Fluid Filter Warning
Causes: Water in the fuel filter has reached maximum capacity
OtherDetails: The driver should drain the water from the fuel filter immediately to prevent water from entering the engine.
